sentences.text: That, that was the wintertime, and we begin, begin the, the temperature begin warmer.
Knowing ?] we decided to what we can do, but it's not possible when the police begin to ask for us, to look for us and more worse it was.
It begin a time, what's begin it was the Ukraine people, they come in the forest.
They was fighting for, for the independent Ukraine.
There was, the politics there, politic it was to kill all other nationalities.
Beside only in Ukraine, must be stay all Ukraine people.
They was killed-- they tried to kill Jewish people.
They tried to kill Russian people.
They tried to kill the Polish people.
And then for us, it begin the worst time.
We got afraid for the police.
We got afraid for the Germans, what we hiding.
And now, begin to hiding from the, from the people what they know us and we know them.
And they know what we, we around them, we stay in the forest around Komarova.
And better of them, but it comes, the people Ukraine what I was together with them.
When I was working in the, in the government business and I went to go to make, to take the money to the bank, no-- and he was always with me.
But this guy, he would-- the guy, he was their head of the, of the bandits.
I will not another word, I cannot.
And he was looking for me what he want to kill me.
How many-- we eat with him together, when we take, take the money at nighttime.
I went with him at nighttime to the bank to come very early, to come back-- because no car seats, only, only with horses.
And, and that guy was looking for me to kill me.
We decided that we will go to the Polish people.
They begin to have a back, a bad time near Komarova, near to this village what he was living.
And maybe a distance it was approximately five kilometers.
It was the Polish people was leaving.
The Ukraine did what they want the Ukraine-- to make their country independent, Ukraine.
They begin to come to the Polish people to kill them, to beat them, to do very bad things.
I will show you, can tell you we got one family in Komarova in the village, when we was in forest, a Polish, a Polish family.
He was [? a real ?] [?
Pole, ?] a [?
Catholic. ?]
He was, he made-- how is?
No, he make from metal some things worse for the people in the, in the village.
They come one night.
They kill him.
They kill the daughter.
The guy what, what told you what he tells us don't came, don't came in this place to the [?
Zabalote. ?] He tell us what they do, what they killed-- the Pol, the Polish man and the daughter.
No-- that's, we, we cannot work or be around them.
We decided to go to the Polish people.
We came one night.
He was not-- he knows better than, than I knows, my friend what [INAUDIBLE] with him.
And we came at nighttime to one guy, a young boy, a Polish, and we talk with him what to do.
We begin to hear about some things partisans is going on around.
No-- we got afraid by ourselves to come out at nighttime to these people.
Because if we will come to the partisans, we'll be OK.
Now, if it will come out to the, [INAUDIBLE] they call [? banderotzi, ?] it was by name what they had there.
It was [? banderotzi, ?] [? milnikotzi. ?] No, the idea, it was the same.
They killed, they killed only the, the names from they had there.
It was a difference.
He tell us, yes, I know the partisans.
They, they come here often-- the Russian partisans, not the Ukraine.
Because the Ukraines, they don't like the Polish and the Polish don't like them.
He tell us what, what day to come.
Come, come and I will meet you with the partisans.
It's [? happening. ?] We come.
And one morning, early, early, we [? come ?] and he was in the forest.
And we see what it comes-- people.
It was maybe, maybe eight persons.
It was a bigger group.

Now, the most group is, was left from this place and went to another place because it was impossible for them to, to stay because it's a lot, a lot of that they call [? banderotzi. ?] And they was fighting with the partisans.
We met with these guys, the four guys-- the eight guys.
We met with them.
Now we explain to them that we're Jewish.
And he done, the, the Polish guy, maybe he's in [INAUDIBLE] or no.
I don't know what happened.
They took him.
He don't know what happened to us.
And they take us with them.
And we went that's near, in the forest near to Manevytskyl.
We left from this place where Komarova.
We told where there's the brother-in-law.
We left him in Komarova.
And we told him [? what ?] if it will be possible he cannot go.
He tell us, you know what, you go.
What it will happen to me?
What they can do, we cannot change.
Then, too, we will, we will be dead [INAUDIBLE] they kill us, all us, three of us, or they will kill me [INAUDIBLE].
And he said, you go.
If it will be possible to come to me to help me, he will come.
And we promise him no, the situation [INAUDIBLE] change in the time, we will come to him.
Then when we go with the guys we came in the forest, and they-- in the way that's-- no, they have the pistols.
They have ammunition.
And we came at day time in a, in a village, another village that's closer to Manevytskyl.
And they borrow, borrow some houses what they know what that's policeman's.
You cannot, I cannot explain the feel-- our feeling, how good it was.
What we cannot by ourselves know.
We, we-- and that's time what we can see, and they was doing what [INAUDIBLE] we was happy.
A better [? quality ?] time, it cannot be.
Then they brought us in a [INAUDIBLE] in forest to a house.
We shaved.
They give us clothes, and we become people.
What some, some of-- we can see a face.
We can see, and I was looking to him and he's looking at me.
How we, how we changed our [INAUDIBLE], and it's become better.
We begin to walk with them to, together in the forest.
They didn't take us because they got afraid for us.
No, I was not in the army because I was young.
And the other guy, he was older.
He was not in the army.
We don't know how to use guns.
I'll tell you I pass one thing.
No.
OK.
I will tell later.
And they didn't take us with them maybe for a couple days.
Then we begin to go with them together.
And, and that time is become more partisans-- [? you know, ?] the part, the partisans that's 1940, 1943.
No, they was in small groups.
Different situation.
It was become-- it was most of Ukraine.
And the name, he was near from the, the, the head of the group partisans.
He was from the next, next village from Komarova, and their last name, it was [?
Shishkor. ?] And the group was calling [?
Shishkotsier ?] in the name, his last name.
When we talk with him, he said, told us, they told us what [? his self, ?] the [?
Shishkor, ?] with a group approximately by 25 persons, maybe 30 persons, they lived in another place.
They went from here, and they stay here.

Then in a week or two weeks, it's-- I [? say ?] what comes, people from other groups to become, to make them in a big, one bigger groups what we can something to do more than it's go around small groups partisans.
No.
That was different.
Was Jewish.
It was Polish.
It was Ukraine.
It was Russian.
It was Armenians-- Armenians.
Different, different.
Because in the partisans it become a lot of Russian soldiers where they was in the camps by the German.
They got very bad time, and they come out.
Then they was begin to fighting.
They're all from the partisans.
I need to say they got a big role in the fight, in the war, very big role.
I will tell you more [? that ?] [? it's ?] beginning on the, [? only ?] from the [? time ?] partisans.
Then it's come a couple-- two, two or three people came from the bigger groups from the partisans to take us.
They know what somebody is here, and they find us.
No, and we tell them, we meet with them, and then tell them what we are Jewish and we want to go with-- together with all, with the group to go.
They took it, they took us, and we came.
That's for a, maybe kilometers-- no. 30 kilometers we make for one night.
And we came in a, in a place that's-- no, I will [? not ?] to tell the episodes, short episodes, how we went through the, the railroad, [INAUDIBLE] we [INAUDIBLE] railroad.
It's, it's-- if to tell episodes, we need time and time and time.
Now, the most important.
I will tell the most important things.
We came in the big forest, that's, that forest, that's more to Belarusian.
That's [? white, ?] [? white ?] Russia [? called. ?] That's more bigger forest than the forest, but we was in that place near to our Komarova.
When we came at daytime, we came in a, in a, a village-- partisans going at daytime.
Don't have afraid nothing.
Oh, we saw we was happy.
They give us food, and we explain them from there.
And we met Jewish people in the partisans.
Then they keep us [? no ?] [? in ?] the [? period. ?] It was different time.
Who knows who, who are you?
Because the Germans, they send people to, to, to, know, to knows what, where is the partisans.
Then they come, they come with, to kill the partisans.
No, they told us, you will stay here for a week in the, in the village.
No.
We begin to help, they tell us.
They give us a gun.
They taught-- they teach us how to use that.
Know ?] that, we become normal people.
If in a situation like this, we know we were doing something.
No, that's you'll be, that you'll be killed, OK?
That's we not by ourselves and not, and not-- we know for what we're going to fight, not what the Germans with the policeman will kill us.
Then we stay maybe for a week in this village.
Then they take us in the main, main office in the forest.
That's we came to, it's called [INAUDIBLE].
That's uncle, uncle-- [? you'll have ?] to translate-- Uncle [?
Petia. ?] Their last name, it was [?
Brinski. ?] [INAUDIBLE] [?
Brinski. ?] When we came to this partisan in the main, main office, no, that was full like, like our [?
Respublika, ?] [? difference ?] [?
Respublik. ?] We met with people.
I met with my two cousins in the same, the same place.
That was-- they came from [INAUDIBLE] ghettos.
They told, they explained to us, both of them, they was killed, killed in the partisans, and [? not ?] [? in ?] [? life. ?] No, it's become another life-- another life and other places.
It was the name, the name of what , where we were standing in the, in the forest.
That's the place, [INAUDIBLE].
But the, the main place what all the partisans will know what, that's, that's the main place for the partisans.
Yes.
That's, that's not in [?
Berlitz. ?] Approximately that's in the border.
Belarus.

Because he was, work, out of work, the, the group was [INAUDIBLE] for only [? diversion. ?] That's mean we blew up railroads, bri-- bridges, main highways, all what that [? stuff ?] [? knew. ?] And we was fighting with some-- with police.
Now, I want to say I'm not the main-- I was not the main person what with-- in the partisans.
Because the problem, I didn't know how to use the, the, didn't know how to blow up.
Now, we was thinking, we was doing different hard jobs for this to make everything in life and make a, a revenge.
A revenge.
We will right the [? world. ?] For the, for the--
Revenge.
[NON-ENGLISH]
Yeah, revenge.
For, to the, for, for, for these people what they dead, and we in life, we want to do it.
For example, the Germans, they put the bombs, big bombs on the partisans, on the army [? of the ?] Russian.
A lot of them didn't blow up and they was standing in the places.
In the partisans, it was a lot of good specialists.
They take off the main piece what it's, what it's, what it's give the power to blow up the bomb.
And we take on, on our wagons, and we bring them to our [? loggers ?] and put them in fire and take off and [INAUDIBLE] become like a liquid.
And we put them in forms and the forms with special, no, pieces what it's blow up.
Then we go to blow up the railroads.
You cannot measure what, what happy we was when we blew up with [?
German's ?] [INAUDIBLE].
This mean a lot of wagons where they, where they take to the, to the front, to the, to the fight with the Russians.
Not only this, we take, they take, they come, the, the Germans.
They take food, some from the, from the people from the Ukraine.
We, at night time, we make, we make places when they go, we take from them back and we give to the people in the Ukraine and [INAUDIBLE], and for us, for the partisans, to feed.
Then we saw that was civilian lager.
It was in the same place, but it was a woman, children, older people-- they didn't went to, to, not to, for fighting, because it's, they cannot do.
No, we bring them food, food a lot.
And it was the group-- it's called [?
Kruk, ?] [?
Kruk. ?] That's most of them, it was a Jewish, a Jewish partisan.
Most of them were Jewish.
We got another, our group, too.
And that group, that was the first partisans was a begin to fight with the Germans.
I think from that group now it's-- I don't know how many people know [? they ?] alive, because they must be in Israel.
No, I don't have now no connection with them.
I would like to, to, to remind the times what we got.
I read a couple, I will tell you later.
I have letters, but not from them, from other ones [? was ?] [?
I ?] [? said. ?] No, what I want to say-- that's life in the partisans.
It's if we will be killed, we will know for what.
It's easier with people.
It's a lot to explain, a lot from the partisans.
And I told one time, and I want to say next one how many good things we was doing the partisans, how much they helped to the, for the army, for the Russian Army.
We take places [INAUDIBLE], for example.
Then, then, then, when [? that's ?] in the 19-- the end of 1943. 1943, yes.
In beginning, 1944.
The Russian Army become to go to the west [? in ?] [? the ?] Germany was running away from them.
And we take some places where the partisans take [? place, ?] for example, we takes-- no, that's not a village, that was a small city.
Like [INAUDIBLE] became the first one.
The partisans, they take, take the city, then it's came to the army.
No, they-- our, our bosses was thinking the army come, the Russian Army begin near and near and near.
No.
What [? your ?] city was doing-- it's a lot what I begin to mixed up the job from the partisans.
The Russian Army begin to send us a lot from the para, the para-- from the airplanes.
From parachute a lot of guns and, and more what that's to, to blow up something.
We went and we take at nighttime.
We put fire, and they put all the stuff.
We take the stuff.
It's a lot, a lot, a lot of work, and we was doing everything [?
OK. ?] Then they decided that the Germans was running back.
Then they tell us what we will go to Poland.
They will send us to Poland, and to fight with the German [? who ?] come in the back from the, from the Germans again, to make territory from Poland.

They took us-- [CLEARS THROAT] the partisans, they took us to the board, to the old board, Poland and Germany and Russia-- to the, to the [? brook. ?] That's the river [? brook. ?] If we cannot come across the river to go in the back to the Germans, to fight them from the back.
We stay maybe a couple days, then it's they decided to send us, all the partisans, to send us in the Russian Army.
They give us the papers, they give us [? new, ?] where to go.
They send us [INAUDIBLE].
That's, that was-- I didn't tell them about the [INAUDIBLE] we was together.
And that time, we divided.
He went in one place, they sent him.
Maybe it was a mistake, our mistake, why we didn't go together to stay, to stay, to ask people to go together.
Or maybe it's another-- I don't know how, what is happening.
He, he was looking like for a Jew.
I was looking, [? but ?] you cannot see.
From outside, you cannot see what I am, what I am a Jew.
And they begin from the partisan, they begin.
They send us an army to teach us for to be [RUSSIAN].
That's mean to come to spy to the Germans to spy.
And it was a special group in the army.
Most of them, they take partisans.
They take people what know don't have relatives, don't have nobody.
Why it was no [? deaths. ?] I didn't know in this time what [? became ?] after.
We understand why it [? was. ?] When they send the people that they send us, that's 95% we went what will be dead.
When we came to the group, they teach us, they teach us maybe that was the city, near the city that's in [INAUDIBLE].
They send us, they teach us how to fight, if you come to the German, how to fight with a knife, how to [? find, ?] how to fight, things like this.
And it's very complicated.
You cannot say what I don't want to do, to do this.
And you was doing with understanding, and you know what can happen.
When we came, a lot of people, they saved us.
But every time when a group goes, the groups, they send six persons, seven persons, and they [INAUDIBLE].
Or they doesn't come back at all, or it's come back a few.
The first time after [? this ?] what I [? told us ?] they give us the uniform of the army.
And the first time when after that, was they teach us how to [INAUDIBLE].
Everything, it was short.
For [INAUDIBLE], they teach us, and they tell [? know ?] what we [? will. ?] Officers didn't give us some uniforms.
No, because we go to there.
You cannot have some papers.
We leave all the papers.
What you have, you cannot take with you.
The first time, it was good.
We brought, we brought one, one German.
And what they was doing, that was not our business.
We bring them, and [INAUDIBLE].
And the second, the second time, that was 1944, maybe June or July, I was wounded.
I went to, to make what that's the job.
And we was seven or eight people.
I was wounded.
And they take us, they take me from [? off, ?] from the, from the place where there became near to the Germans.
And they take us, take me, to a hospital.
It was close to the hospital.
They put some-- something with the blood.
I got wounded in the foot.
And in the hospital--
